definitions.failureMessage: manifest.json "description" exceeds the Edge Add-ons limit of 132 characters.
definitions.fix: Shorten the "description" field in manifest.json to 132 characters or fewer. The full marketing description belongs in the store listing, not the manifest.

definitions.failureMessage: JSON does not match all schemas of AllOf - invalid schema indexes
definitions.fix: Edge Add-ons reports this when manifest.json fails the composite (AllOf) schema. Common causes: (1) "manifest_version" is a string instead of the number 3; (2) MV3 "web_accessible_resources" is a flat array of paths instead of [{ "resources": [...], "matches": [...] }]; (3) "content_security_policy" is a string (MV2) in an MV3 manifest - it must be an object { "extension_pages": "..." }; (4) "background" mixes MV2 "scripts" with MV3 "service_worker". Fix each to match MV3 exactly, then re-submit.

"invalid schema indexes" means one or more fields failed their sub-schema. Check: "permissions" must only list API names ("<all_urls>" belongs in "host_permissions"); "host_permissions" must be an array of match patterns, not a single string; "icons" keys must be numeric strings ("16", "48", "128"); every "matches" / "include_globs" / "exclude_globs" must be an array. Validate your manifest against the Chrome MV3 JSON schema before re-submitting.

Yellow Potassium = minimum functionality. The reviewer found the extension too simple, broken, or indistinguishable from a bookmark. Add real standalone value (settings UI, keyboard shortcuts, multi-step workflow), remove placeholder screens, and rewrite the store description to explain why it's more than a shortcut.

Purple Potassium = excessive permissions. Every permission must be justified in the store listing's Permission Justification fields AND actively used in code. Remove unused permissions; for the remainder, add a one-sentence justification tied to a specific feature. Prefer "activeTab" over "tabs" when possible, and declarativeNetRequest over webRequest.

Yellow Lithium = Spam. Common triggers: (1) the store description / title contains keyword-stuffed lists, (2) multiple near-identical listings from the same developer, (3) repetitive installs of the same extension under different skins. De-duplicate listings, rewrite the description as prose, and remove non-functional keyword dumps.

Gold Magnesium = deceptive installation or behavior. Typically cited when the extension surprises the user (injects ads, opens tabs, changes search/new tab) without clear opt-in inside the extension UI. Add an unmistakable first-run consent screen, never auto-enable surprising behaviors, and document every side effect in the listing.

Purple Inkberry = misleading trademarks / metadata. Extensions cannot imply endorsement by a third party. Remove trademarked brand names from the extension name (ok in description with disclaimer), use your own branding, and add "Not affiliated with <Trademark>" where reasonable.

definitions.failureMessage: Requesting but not using the following permission(s)
definitions.fix: The reviewer detected a permission declared in manifest.json that never appears in any code path. Either (1) remove the unused permission, or (2) add the API call (e.g. chrome.tabs.query, chrome.bookmarks.getTree, chrome.cookies.get) in a code path that actually executes. Re-run static search with grep/rg to confirm each permission is genuinely exercised.

Firefox 140+ requires the built-in data-consent manifest. Under browser_specific_settings.gecko, add a "data_collection_permissions" object per the Firefox add-on data classification taxonomy (e.g. { "required": ["technicalAndInteraction"], "optional": ["locationInfo"] }) - or explicitly declare { "required": ["none"] } if you collect nothing. See https://extensionworkshop.com/documentation/develop/firefox-builtin-data-consent/

definitions.failureMessage: Add-ons are not allowed to contain obfuscated code
definitions.fix: AMO distinguishes minification (allowed) from obfuscation (banned). Remove packed string arrays (var _0x...=[]), P-A-C-K-E-R output (eval(function(p,a,c,k,e..., and large String.fromCharCode arrays. Ship normal minified output (terser without name mangling for module exports) and submit the original source via the source-code form.

definitions.failureMessage: Consent experience is not unmissable (popup window rejection)
definitions.fix: Mozilla explicitly rejects data-consent dialogs presented as popup windows because they can be missed or hidden. Present consent in a focused new tab (chrome.tabs.create) on install, include all options on a single page, and never use multi-step decline flows or reduced-contrast fonts.

AMO bans cryptocurrency miners outright - there is no opt-in exception. Remove every miner library (Coinhive, CryptoNight, CoinIMP, miner.js, etc.) and any WebAssembly module performing hash computations, then re-submit.

definitions.failureMessage: Modifying web content to include affiliate promotion tags is not permitted
definitions.fix: Injecting affiliate codes into links the user is already clicking on (Amazon ?tag=, eBay ?mpre=, etc.) is a hard-rejection on AMO. Affiliate revenue is only allowed when the link appears inside UI clearly owned by the extension (options page, popup button), not when it rewrites third-party page content.

definitions.failureMessage: Metadata references other browsers or platforms (2.3.10)
definitions.fix: App Store guideline 2.3.10 bans references to competing platforms. Strip Chrome/Firefox/etc. from screenshots, description, What's New, in-app strings, and website bundle. Replace "Available on Chrome" text with "Also available on other browsers" without naming them.
